shared_properties: Vent footprint stays 16x16px to sit cleanly on the shaft tile grid.
Dungeon palette remains low-saturation with the airflow cue isolated to a brighter accent cluster.
Outer outline stays 1px thick for clear obstacle readability against dark backgrounds.
differences: Sprite sheet is 64x16px with four glow frames that cycle downward airflow streaks.
Center nozzle pixels brighten toward #4A90A4 and pale amber to communicate added falling force.
Interior motion emphasizes vertical pull instead of horizontal gusting so the mechanic stays explicit.

context.usage: Force field vent that accelerates descent when the player passes through its lane.

shape.silhouette: Square vent grille with a bright inner aperture and airflow streaks.
shape.collision_read: The active force origin stays centered in the 16x16 cell for precise lane reading.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet size is exactly 64x16px with four 16x16px frames.
Airflow streaks read as downward force in every frame without horizontal ambiguity.
Glow pixels stay contained inside the vent silhouette so the gameplay lane remains crisp.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle on a vent entity paired with a separate force-volume component.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and attach the atlas to wind vent prefabs.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Drive gameplay motion in FixedUpdate at 60 FPS and keep art timing on deterministic timers.
Keep acceleration magnitude in gameplay code and use the sprite only to signal vent presence.
